cases.title: Organizer Admin creates event with basic information in Draft state
preconditions: Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor with organization scope
Target organization exists in organizations table
Valid ruleConfig with non-negative capacity and chronologically consistent registration, check-in, and feedback windows
steps: Invoke eventService.create with name, description, startAt, endAt, location, organizationId, and ruleConfig within a real DB transaction
Read persisted event entity and linked EventRuleConfig snapshot from the database
cases.expected: Event is created with state Draft; returned snapshot includes eventId, organizationId (organizing unit), name, description, location, startAt, endAt, createdBy matching the admin actor, and associated ruleConfig; satisfies Workflow A step 1 (admin creates event in Draft) and Event Module responsibility for event creation

cases.title: POST /api/v1/events returns Draft event with all basic fields
preconditions: Valid OrganizerAdmin JWT scoped to the organization
Request body includes name, description, startAt, endAt, location, organizationId, and valid ruleConfig
steps: POST /api/v1/events with Authorization bearer token and JSON body containing basic event information
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} with the same organizer token
cases.expected: POST returns HTTP 200 with versioned entity snapshot: state Draft, name, description, location, startAt, endAt, organizationId, and eventId; GET detail returns the same basic fields per API design §2.1 Event Management

cases.title: Organizer Admin creates event via create/edit form with basic fields
preconditions: Web app running with organizer authenticated as OrganizerAdmin
Organizer navigates to event create page
steps: Fill event name, description, start/end time, location, and organizing unit (if exposed in form)
Submit save/create draft action
Observe confirmation and event detail or list entry
cases.expected: UI shows newly created draft event with entered name, description, time, location, and organizing unit visible on detail or list view without data loss

cases.title: Event creation establishes initial Draft lifecycle state
preconditions: Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor
Valid create payload with all required basic fields and ruleConfig
No prior event row for the new eventId
steps: Invoke eventService.create with complete basic information
Read event.state before and after the create command
cases.expected: beforeState: no event entity; afterState: Draft; initial lifecycle state matches Workflow A step 1 and Event Module guardrail that new events begin in Draft before publish transitions

cases.title: Flow A lifecycle begins with draft event creation
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
E2E fixture organization seeded
Valid ruleConfig suitable for subsequent publish and open-registration steps
steps: POST /api/v1/events with basic information to create Draft event
Verify response state is Draft before proceeding to publish in Flow A
cases.expected: Created event is in Draft state with complete basic metadata (name, description, time, location, organizing unit), enabling testing-plan Flow A steps 2–4 (configure rules, validate completeness, publish)

cases.title: OrganizerStaff and Participant cannot create events
preconditions: Authenticated JWT for OrganizerStaff and separately for Participant
Otherwise valid POST /api/v1/events body with basic fields
steps: POST /api/v1/events with OrganizerStaff bearer token
POST /api/v1/events with Participant bearer token
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 unauthorized; no Draft event is created; permission matrix Create/edit event draft is OrganizerAdmin only per roles-permissions §2

cases.title: Unauthenticated POST /events is rejected before domain execution
preconditions: No Authorization header on request
Valid create-event JSON body
steps: POST /api/v1/events without bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 401 unauthenticated response; event creation handler is not invoked; aligns with API auth-required principle for non-public endpoints

cases.title: POST /events rejects missing or blank event name
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
Payload with name absent, empty, or whitespace-only; all other fields otherwise valid
steps: POST /api/v1/events with invalid name field
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_INPUT and message indicating name is required; no Draft event created per API validation guardrails §5

cases.title: POST /events rejects startAt on or after endAt
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
Payload with valid name and ruleConfig but startAt equal to or later than endAt
steps: POST /api/v1/events with invalid schedule (startAt >= endAt)
cases.expected: HTTP 422 semantic rule violation indicating startAt must be before endAt; no event row persisted; aligns with Workflow A guardrail on chronological window consistency

cases.title: Create event rejects unknown organizing unit organizationId
preconditions: Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor
organizationId UUID that does not exist in organizations table
Otherwise valid basic event payload
steps: Invoke eventService.create with the unknown organizationId
cases.expected: Request fails with typed API error (404 or 400) referencing invalid organizationId; no event row persisted in database

cases.title: Create validates input strictly before persisting to database
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin actor context
Create payload with invalid ruleConfig window where registrationOpenAt >= registrationCloseAt
steps: Attempt eventService.create with invalid ruleConfig within a DB transaction
Query events table for a row matching the attempted name
cases.expected: Validation fails with INVALID_INPUT or semantic 422 before any INSERT; no partial event or rule_config rows committed; API principle strict input validation before domain execution upheld

cases.title: POST /events rejects malformed ISO-8601 timestamps in time fields
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
Payload with name and location set but startAt or endAt is not valid ISO-8601
steps: POST /api/v1/events with invalid startAt or endAt string
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with code INVALID_INPUT and field-level details; no Draft event created

cases.title: Event module enforces RBAC at service boundary for create command
preconditions: Actor context with role other than OrganizerAdmin (e.g. OrganizerStaff)
Valid create payload with basic fields
steps: Invoke create command through event module with non-admin actor
Observe authorization guard outcome and database state
cases.expected: Access denied at API or service guard without mutating state; no event row inserted; business invariants remain in domain services per module breakdown design principles and authorization decision pattern steps 1–2
